title: boechera davidsonii
intro: Boechera davidsonii, also known as Davidson's rockcress, is a perennial plant species belonging to the family Brassicaceae. It is a flowering plant native to the western United States, particularly in the mountainous regions of California and Nevada. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can be attributed to the presence of glucosinolates and other bioactive compounds. Boechera davidsonii can be used in herbal preparations, such as teas, tinctures, and salves, to aid in the treatment of various health issues, including wounds, skin conditions, and respiratory problems.
